title: anthriscus sylvestris var. alpina
intro: Anthriscus sylvestris var. alpina, also known as Wild Chervil, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it useful in treating digestive issues and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Wild Chervil include relieving symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as soothing digestive problems like bloating and gas. Wild Chervil can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried and used in capsules, making it a versatile remedy for various health concerns.
